subtitle: the white gold wielder · ten books, three Chronicles
author: Stephen R. Donaldson
note: The full Covenant Chronicles sorted by Hero, Anti-Hero, and Grey — with the Wielder and the Despiser set apart as the bound axis of all ten books. roster.json is the source of truth. Characters by Stephen R. Donaldson, inspiration only; the Chronicles' hardest material is treated here as archetype, not detail. Every ACI now carries the full DLW tag with an authored six-W .spun.

classes.label: The Wielder & The Despiser
classes.spec: the bound center of all ten books

members.name: Lord Mhoram
members.specialty: High Lord of the Council; wisdom and compassion that refuse despair even at the end. The steady heart of the First Chronicles.
members.who: High Lord of the Council of Lords at Revelstone, seer and teacher.
members.what: The steady moral heart of the First Chronicles' defenders of the Land.
members.where: Revelstone and the councils and battlefields of the war against the Despiser.
members.why: Because wisdom and compassion must refuse despair, even when the end seems certain.
members.when: Through the long siege and trials of the First Chronicles.
members.how: By insight, patience, and a courage that finds hope where others find only ruin.

members.name: Saltheart Foamfollower
members.specialty: Giant; Covenant's truest friend; joy, story, and the caamora. Gives himself to the fire so the Land might live — the purest love in the Chronicles.
members.who: A Giant of Seareach, storyteller, and Covenant's truest friend.
members.what: The purest love in the Chronicles, given wholly to the Land.
members.where: From the homes of the Giants to the deepest places of Covenant's final ordeal.
members.why: Because friendship and the joy of story are worth any sacrifice.
members.when: Across the First Chronicles, to its culminating fire.
members.how: Through joy, laughter, and the caamora — giving himself to the fire so the Land might live.

members.name: Pitchwife
members.specialty: Giant of the Search; deformed and gentle; loves Gossamer Glowlimn and sings cracked stone whole.
members.who: A Giant of the Search, deformed in body and gentle in spirit.
members.what: The mender and singer who keeps the Search whole in heart and hull.
members.where: Aboard Starfare's Gust and across the seas of the Second Chronicles.
members.why: For love of Gossamer Glowlimn and devotion to his companions.
members.when: Throughout the long voyage of the Giants' Search.
members.how: By singing cracked stone whole and answering hardship with humor and craft.

members.name: The First of the Search
members.specialty: Gossamer Glowlimn; Swordmain and leader of the Giants' Search; iron, honor, and a long faith.
members.who: Gossamer Glowlimn, Swordmain and leader of the Giants' Search.
members.what: The iron-willed commander who holds the Search to its long purpose.
members.where: At the head of the Search, from the Giants' homeland across distant waters.
members.why: To keep faith with an ancient charge and see the Land's peril met.
members.when: Through the seafaring quest of the Second Chronicles.
members.how: By honor, discipline, and a faith that endures every doubt.

members.name: Sunder & Hollian
members.specialty: Graveler and eh-Brand of the Sunbane age; ordinary folk who carry the Land's hope across a broken time into the future.
members.who: A Graveler and an eh-Brand, ordinary folk of the Sunbane age.
members.what: The bearers who carry the Land's hope across a broken time.
members.where: Among the Stonedown and Woodhelven villages under the Sunbane.
members.why: Because even common people must shoulder the Land's future when the great are gone.
members.when: During the ravaged years of the Second Chronicles.
members.how: By the old village powers of stone and lianar, and stubborn human endurance.

members.name: Stave
members.specialty: Haruchai cast out by the Masters for choosing Linden over their pride; loyalty that outgrows his people's iron law.
members.who: A Haruchai cast out by the Masters for his choice.
members.what: Loyalty that outgrows the iron law of his own people.
members.where: At Linden Avery's side through the Last Chronicles.
members.why: Because he chooses Linden over the pride and judgment of the Masters.
members.when: Across the events of the Last Chronicles.
members.how: Through unflinching Haruchai strength tempered at last by humility.

members.name: The Ranyhyn
members.specialty: the great horses of Ra; proud and free; keep ancient promises no human could honor, across centuries.
members.who: The great free horses of Ra.
members.what: Keepers of ancient promises no human could honor.
members.where: The plains of Ra and wherever their oaths call them across the Land.
members.why: To keep faith freely given, beyond compulsion or fear.
members.when: Across all the Chronicles, over centuries.
members.how: By pride, freedom, and the unbroken keeping of their word.

members.name: Berek Halfhand
members.specialty: the Lord-Fatherer of legend; founder of the line of Lords and the Staff of Law; the lineage's headwaters.
members.who: The Lord-Fatherer of legend, founder of the line of Lords.
members.what: The headwaters of the lineage that shapes the Land's history.
members.where: In the ancient days at the root of the Land's age of Lords.
members.why: To answer ruin with the founding of Law and Earthpower.
members.when: In the deep past, the legend behind all that follows.
members.how: By forging the Staff of Law and the line of Lords from sacrifice.

members.name: Linden Avery
members.specialty: the Chosen; a physician who Sees the Land's health and sickness. Saves through love — and damns through it: raises the dead, wakes the Worm of the World's End. The Second and Last Chronicles are hers.
members.who: The Chosen, a physician who Sees the Land's health and sickness.
members.what: The one whose love both saves the Land and imperils it.
members.where: Throughout the Land of the Second and Last Chronicles, which are hers.
members.why: Out of a fierce love that will not let go of those she would heal.
members.when: Across the Second and Last Chronicles.
members.how: By her health-sense and, fatefully, by power turned to raising the dead and waking the Worm of the World's End.

members.name: High Lord Elena
members.specialty: Covenant and Lena's daughter; High Lord whose love and vengeance break the Law of Death; power bent to ruin with the best of intentions.
members.who: Covenant and Lena's daughter, raised to High Lord.
members.what: Power bent to ruin with the best of intentions.
members.where: At Revelstone and in the forbidden reaches beyond the Law of Death.
members.why: Out of love and a vengeance she cannot master.
members.when: During the First Chronicles' war against the Despiser.
members.how: By breaking the Law of Death and so turning her own strength against the Land.

members.name: Hile Troy
members.specialty: blind warmark from Covenant's world who can see only within the Land; spends everything — and at last his very self — for command, and becomes Caer-Caveral.
members.who: A blind warmark from Covenant's world who can see only within the Land.
members.what: The commander who spends everything — at last his very self.
members.where: On the battlefields of the Land during the First Chronicles.
members.why: To win the war and prove worthy of the sight the Land grants him.
members.when: Across the campaigns of the First Chronicles, and beyond.
members.how: By absolute commitment to command, surrendering himself to become Caer-Caveral.

members.name: The Haruchai
members.specialty: the Bloodguard, later the Masters; absolute devotion curdled to pride; their unbending Vow and rule do nearly as much harm as good.
members.who: The Bloodguard, and later the Masters of the Land.
members.where: From their mountain home to their long watch over the Land.
members.why: To serve and guard by an unbending standard of their own making.
members.how: Through their Vow and their rule — which do nearly as much harm as good.

members.name: The Elohim
members.specialty: beautiful, amoral, self-regarding powers; the Würd of the Earth made flesh; they aid and obstruct by lights no mortal can read.
members.who: Beautiful, amoral, self-regarding powers, the Würd of the Earth made flesh.
members.what: Aid and obstruction by lights no mortal can read.
members.where: In Elemesnedene and wherever their inscrutable concerns reach.
members.why: For purposes wholly their own, beyond mortal morality.
members.when: Chiefly in the Second and Last Chronicles.
members.how: By powers and designs that help and hinder in the same gesture.

members.name: The Insequent
members.specialty: the Mahdoubt, the Harrow, the Theomach; solitary masters of vast lore, each serving only their own long purpose.
members.who: The Mahdoubt, the Harrow, the Theomach — solitary masters of vast lore.
members.what: Bearers of immense knowledge, each serving only their own long purpose.
members.where: Wandering the margins of the Land, apart from its peoples.
members.why: Each for an aim entirely their own.
members.how: By singular, jealously held lore and cunning.

members.name: Kevin Landwaster
members.specialty: High Lord whose despair loosed the Ritual of Desecration and broke the Land; the cautionary grey at the root of all its grief.
members.who: The High Lord whose despair broke the Land.
members.what: The cautionary grey at the root of all the Land's grief.
members.where: At Revelstone and across the Land he ruled.
members.why: Out of a despair the Despiser nursed and turned against him.
members.when: In the age before the First Chronicles, casting its shadow forward.
members.how: By loosing the Ritual of Desecration that laid the Land waste.

members.name: Anele
members.specialty: blind, mad earth-seer; last of an old line; truth wears him like a borrowed coat and sets him down again.
members.who: A blind, mad earth-seer, last of an old line.
members.what: A vessel through whom truth speaks and departs.
members.where: Wandering the Land of the Last Chronicles.
members.why: Driven by a burden of inheritance and loss he cannot set down.
members.how: By earth-sight and the way truth wears him like a borrowed coat and sets him down again.

members.name: Esmer
members.specialty: Cail's son, get of the merewives; torn forever between aiding and betraying — harm and help in the same breath. Grey made flesh.
members.who: Cail's son, get of the merewives, grey made flesh.
members.what: Harm and help offered in the same breath.
members.where: At the edges of Linden's path through the Last Chronicles.
members.why: Torn forever between the loyalties and betrayals of his divided birth.
members.how: By aiding and betraying at once, unable to be wholly either.

members.name: Thomas Covenant
members.specialty: the white gold wielder. A leper exiled from his own world; the Unbeliever who will not grant the Land is real. Guilty of an unforgivable act in the first days, and driven by that guilt ever after. Wild magic — keystone of the Arch of Time — answers his ring; his refusal to surrender it is the only thing that holds. The anti-hero on whom everything turns.
members.who: The white gold wielder — a leper exiled from his own world, the Unbeliever.
members.what: The anti-hero on whom everything turns, keystone of the Arch of Time.
members.where: Caught between his own world and the Land across all ten books.
members.why: Driven by guilt for an unforgivable act in the first days, and by a refusal to surrender.
members.when: From the First Chronicles to the Last Dark — the bound axis of the whole saga.
members.how: By wild magic answering his ring, and by the refusal to give it up that alone holds the Arch.

members.name: Lord Foul the Despiser
members.specialty: the bitterness at the heart of the world — Despiser, Corruption, Fangthane. He whispers 'you are mine,' for he is Covenant's own despair given a voice: the Land's destroyer, and the dark proof of its meaning.
members.who: The bitterness at the heart of the world — Despiser, Corruption, Fangthane.
members.what: The Land's destroyer, and the dark proof of its meaning.
members.where: From his strongholds to every corner of the Land he would unmake.
members.why: To break the Arch of Time and be free of the world that binds him.
members.when: Behind every Chronicle, the bound center opposite the Wielder.
members.how: By whispering 'you are mine' — for he is Covenant's own despair given a voice.
